title: John 6:40
message: For my Father's will is that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in him shall have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
notes: The Father's will is eternal life for everyone who believes — not a possibility, a certainty. Jesus personally guarantees the resurrection. My future is not uncertain; it is held in the will of God Himself.

title: 1 John 2:17
message: The world and its desires pass away, but whoever does the will of God lives forever.
notes: Everything that is not God is temporary. The world's systems, its appeal, its momentum — all of it is passing. I align myself with the only thing that does not expire: the will of God.

title: Isaiah 40:8
message: The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the word of our God endures forever.
notes: Everything in the natural order has an expiry — except the Word of God. Every promise I stand on is backed by something that cannot wither. I build my life on the only foundation that is eternally stable.

title: Matthew 24:35
message: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
notes: Jesus places His words above the permanence of the physical universe. The cosmos will be remade; His word will not be revised. What He has said about me, about my future, and about His kingdom stands forever.

title: Psalm 119:89
message: Your word, LORD, is eternal; it stands firm in the heavens.
notes: God's word is already settled in heaven — not being debated, not subject to revision. Before I ever encounter a promise in scripture, it has already been standing firm in the heavens. I am agreeing with what is already established.

title: Romans 8:38-39
message: For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
notes: Paul exhausts every category of existence and finds nothing that can break this one truth: God's love in Christ is inseparable from me. This is not a seasonal comfort — it is an eternal constant that no dimension of reality can override.

title: John 3:16
message: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
notes: The axis of all eternity — love expressed as a gift, received through faith, resulting in a life that does not end. This verse is not just an entry point to faith; it is the eternal truth the entire gospel stands on.

title: Hebrews 13:8
message: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
notes: He does not evolve, does not update, does not change with the times. The Christ who healed, who provided, who raised the dead — that is the same Christ I have access to today. His character is an eternal constant.

title: 1 Peter 1:25
message: But the word of the Lord endures forever. And this is the word that was preached to you.
notes: The same word that endures forever is the word that was preached to me. I did not receive a temporary message — I received an eternal one. The gospel I believe in has the same shelf life as God Himself.

title: Malachi 3:6
message: I the LORD do not change. So you, the descendants of Jacob, are not destroyed.
notes: God's unchanging nature is the reason I am still standing. His constancy is not just a theological fact — it is the practical reason I survive every season intact. What He promised, He is still committed to. What He began, He has not abandoned.
